name: eth_deposit_per_block_state_drop
description: Issues 200 successive L1->L2 ETH deposits (each in its own block) and asserts the recipient is credited at block 1 with the first deposit value. arbreth fails: balance reads as 0 at block 1 because the direct cache writes from mint_balance/transfer_balance leak forward through the in_memory_state pipeline and are only "applied" once the cumulative bundle flushes to MDBX (default ARB_FLUSH_INTERVAL=128 blocks). The single-block variant passes — only the multi-block case hits the leak. Reproduces the per-block stateRoot divergence on the live node at Sepolia block 84,449,584.
